domain: statistical mechanics (Loschmidt paradox, microreversibility, fluctuation theorems)
specific_mechanism: microreversibility-based impossibility argument: deterministic-trajectory information cannot be erased without exporting entropy to an external bath
llm_application: claim: gradient-descent-trained LLM weights cannot achieve information-theoretic forgetting of training data under any local unlearning algorithm; forgetting is necessarily 'apparent', recoverable by an adversary with small fine-tuning budget; only weight-bath exchange (full retraining from scratch) can be irreversible
novelty_claim: Stronger claim than current 'unlearning is reversible' empirical results: a *principled* impossibility proof grounded in the Loschmidt/microreversibility argument. The candidate predicts that any local unlearning method on a deterministic optimizer will leave information recoverable in the orthogonal directions to the local perturbation.
